user_class.convention: Bean-Getter = a,b,c,… in Feld-Reihenfolge, Setter = ab i()

ble.encrypt: frame = data + randomHex; frame += CRC; ct = AES/CBC/PKCS7(hexToBytes(frame), K, IV=randomIV); out = hex(randomIV ++ ct)
ble.decrypt: iv = data[:16]; pt = AES/CBC/PKCS7_decrypt(data[16:], K, iv); muss mit 'DFED' beginnen, CRC prüfen

jc.role: MQTT-Connection-Manager, f() baut Credentials, y() connectet

fields.body: JSON-Payload (häufig mit Feld cmd), verschlüsselt via Schicht C

third_party_mqtt.note: userName/password/token werden vor Versand über Lbb/c;->d(String) verarbeitet (Klasse fehlt)

mqtt.payload_encryption_note: body im MQTT-Envelope ist Base64-String (AES-128-CBC/PKCS7 mit bluetoothKey), nicht rohes JSON

common_request_pattern.note: Geräte-Stats nutzen deviceId/deviceSn, System-Stats nutzen systemId

common_response_pattern.format: { x:[Labels], y:[Werte], y1, y2, y3, y4, unit, total… }
common_response_pattern.description: x = Achsenbeschriftung (Zeit), y… = Datenreihen, unit = Einheit, total… = Summen

flow: 2. WebView-Login bei Shelly; Redirect auf Callback-URL

steps: Sensoren für SystemBody, PV, CT, Batteriepack, Steckdosen
Keine aktiven Commands bis Topic- und Ack-Verhalten dokumentiert

end_to_end_flow: 1. Login (Schicht A): AES-Key generieren → JSON AES/ECB → AES-Key RSA-wrappen → POST auth/login → token + mqttPassWord
2. Geräteliste GET device/bind/list → pro Gerät devSn, bluetoothKey, devType, modelCode
3. MQTT-Connect (Schicht B): Credentials aus mqttPassWord → TLS zu emqx.jackeryapp.com:8883
4. Subscribe hb/app/<devSn>/device; body (Schicht C) mit Base64.decode(bluetoothKey) als AES-128-CBC/PKCS7 (IV=Key) entschlüsseln → JSON-Telemetrie
5. Steuern: Command-JSON → Schicht C verschlüsseln → Envelope body → publish hb/app/<devSn>/command
open_items: exakte Feld-Einheiten/Skalierung (W vs. 0.1W, % vs. 0.1%) per Capture kalibrieren

external_library_requirements: All communication to external devices/services must be wrapped in external Python library hosted on PyPI
Library must have source distribution packages available (no binary-only packages)
Issue trackers must be enabled for external libraries
New dependencies added to requirements_all.txt via: python3 -m script.gen_requirements_all
New codeowners added to CODEOWNERS via: python3 -m script.hassfest
.strict-typ file updated if fully type hinted

common: Only add new constants to const.py if widely used
external_requirements: Requirements added to manifest.json (REQUIREMENTS constant deprecated)
configuration: Voluptuous schema present for configuration validation
Default parameters specified in voluptuous schema, not in setup()
Schema using generic config keys from homeassistant.const
If component has platforms, define PLATFORM_SCHEMA instead of CONFIG_SCHEMA
Never depend on users adding things to customize
communication: Share data with platforms via hass.data[DOMAIN]
Use dispatcher code in homeassistant.helpers.dispatcher for updates
device_communication: All API specific code must be part of third party library hosted on PyPI
Home Assistant should only interact with objects, not make direct API calls
GOOD: from phue import Bridge; bridge = Bridge(...); status = bridge.status()

setup: Group calls to add_entities if possible
entity: Extend entity from integration being built for
Avoid passing hass as parameter (set when entity added to HA)
Do not call update() in constructor, use add_entities(devices, update_before_add=True)
Do not do I/O inside properties, cache values in update()
Store UTC timestamps, not relative time

authentication.purpose: Acquire authentication tokens and make authenticated requests
requirements: Should acquire tokens, update if necessary, use for requests
Return authentication in JSON serializable format (dict with primitive types)
Allow developer to pass API location if served from multiple locations

Auth.__init__: websession: ClientSession, host: str, access_token: str
Auth.request: async def request(method: str, path: str, **kwargs) -> ClientResponse
def request(method: str, path: str, **kwargs) -> requests.Response

oauth2.description: Standardized authentication using refresh and access tokens

async_pattern.implementation: Developer implements async_get_access_token with token manager

Auth.request: def request(method: str, path: str, **kwargs) -> Response

principles: Expose data in same structure as API offers
Do not transform data (e.g., no Celsius/Fahrenheit conversion)
Let developer using library handle transformations

structure.properties: Map to API field names (id, name, is_on)

basic_library_requirements: Versions correspond to tagged releases in public repository
Publishing on PyPI must be automated
OSI-approved license for library and subdependencies

required_fields.domain: Short name with characters and underscores, must be unique, matches directory
required_fields.name: Integration name (append 'Cloud' for cloud variants, no suffix for local)

optional_fields.version: Required for custom integrations (CalVer or SemVer)
optional_fields.dependencies: Other integrations that must be set up first
optional_fields.after_dependencies: Optional dependencies that might be used
optional_fields.config_flow: true if integration has config flow
optional_fields.single_config_entry: true if only one config entry allowed
optional_fields.loggers: List of logger names used by requirements

rules: Name property identifies data point, not device or entity type
Main feature of device: name = None
friendly_name = device.name + entity.name (or just device.name if entity.name is None)

methods: Entity description: entity_description attribute with EntityDescription instance

lifecycle_hooks.async_added_to_hass: Called when entity_id and hass assigned, before first write
lifecycle_hooks.async_will_remove_from_hass: Called when entity about to be removed

recorder_exclusions.description: Exclude attributes not suitable for history recording

best_practices: Add bluetooth_adapters to dependencies in manifest.json
Call bluetooth.async_get_scanner API to get BleakScanner instance
Use connection timeout of at least 10 seconds
Use bleak-retry-connector package for reliable connections

connectable_modes.connectable_false: Device only needs advertisement data, receives from all controllers

PassiveBluetoothProcessorCoordinator.use_case: Primary method is BLE advertisements, primary function is sensor/binary_sensor/events
PassiveBluetoothProcessorCoordinator.description: Minimal code needed, frameworks create entities on demand

ActiveBluetoothProcessorCoordinator.description: Like Passive but with needs_poll_method and poll_method

PassiveBluetoothCoordinator.description: Similar to DataUpdateCoordinator but driven by advertisements

ActiveBluetoothDataUpdateCoordinator.use_case: Active BLE connections, not primarily sensor
ActiveBluetoothDataUpdateCoordinator.description: Like Passive but with polling capability

DataUpdateCoordinator.use_case: Device only uses active BLE connection, no advertisements

device_tracking.async_track_unavailable: Get callback when device no longer seen
device_tracking.async_scanner_devices_by_address: Get devices by address from all adapters

external_scanners.async_get_advertisement_callback: Get callback to feed advertisement data

performance_considerations.match_history: Do not clear for frequently changing data (sensors)
performance_considerations.advertisement_history: Clear after GATT session ends to receive wake-up advertisements

show_form.sections: vol.Schema with section() for grouping fields

show_menu.menu_options: List or dict of step_id: label

validation.description: Validate user input after schema validation
validation.errors: Dict with field names as keys, translation keys as values
validation.base_errors: Use 'base' key for errors unrelated to specific field

multi_step_flows.pattern: Store info in self, return await self.async_step_next()

yaml_import.note: New integrations should not accept credentials in configuration.yaml

lookup_helpers.async_get_discovery_info_by_udn_st: Single device by UDN and ST

network.returns: List of adapter dicts with ipv4/ipv6 info

usb.async_is_plugged_in: Check if specific USB adapter present
usb.async_register_scan_request_callback: Callback when new USB devices available

authentication_providers.description: Confirm user identity via login flow
authentication_providers.note: Currently only built-in auth providers supported

features: Establish connection and authenticate with HA instance
Call service actions, fire events, render templates
View to control house via authenticated webview

services_api.description: Internal services API for service discovery

logger_management.warning: KRITISCH: Logger NICHT löschen ohne Code vorher zu prüfen
logger_management.best_practice: Logger beibehalten, nur Log-Level anpassen wenn nötig
